chapter_title.en: The Virtue of Mosques, Their Sanctity and the Reward for Those Who Pray in Them

parts.text: سَأَلَ عَبْدُ اَلْأَعْلَى مَوْلَى آلِ سَامٍ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: كَمْ كَانَ طُولُ مَسْجِدِ رَسُولِ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ

text: «كَانَ ثَلاَثَةَ آلاَفٍ وَ سِتَّمِائَةِ ذِرَاعٍ مُكَسَّرَةً.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.683 - Abd al-Ala, the freedman of Ale Saam, asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about the dimensions of the Mosque of the Messenger of Allah (sw).
Imam (as) said: "It was three thousand and six hundred cubits, broken up (referring to its measured parts)”.
